doom.driver: Europe could run out of jet fuel in 6 weeks, IEA chief tells AP.
doom.blurb: EUROPE GROUNDS ITSELF IN SIX WEEKS
doom.summary: The IEA chief warning Europe faces jet fuel exhaustion in six weeks is the Strait of Hormuz crisis graduating from 'expensive inconvenience' to 'civilizational logistics problem.' Meanwhile Russia hammered Ukraine with 700 drones — its biggest attack in weeks — just to remind everyone that the other war is still very much on. The Iran ceasefire remains 'fragile' per the BBC's on-the-ground reporting, which is diplomat-speak for 'a stiff breeze could end it.'

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran ceasefire holding but described as fragile, while Europe faces a 6-week jet fuel cliff and the Hormuz blockade traps Gulf energy with no resolution in sight.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Trump threatening to fire Fed Chair Powell injects new financial instability; IMF flags a global 'guns vs. butter' crisis as defense spending crowds out social investment.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: TSMC posts 58% profit surge on AI demand and Anthropic announces UK expansion — capability buildout continues at pace but no alarming leaps today.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: Maine passes a moratorium on AI data centers and Adobe pivots to conversational AI editing — governance is lagging capability but no acute alignment crisis visible.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A major Australian refinery fire compounding global fuel shortages, plus Russia's largest drone/missile assault on Ukraine in weeks, add compounding fragility to an already stressed system.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, extreme close-up filling the entire frame on a jet fuel gauge mounted on a massive product tanker's deck instrumentation panel, the needle buried hard into the red empty zone, the circular dial face cracked and filmed with salt haze, dark crimson reflections pooling across the scratched glass face of the gauge — the needle itself cast in deep charcoal shadow with only its tip catching a last sliver of firelight-orange glow, the surrounding panel of riveted grey steel streaked with rust bleeding downward like old wounds, at the extreme edges of the frame the blurred suggestion of the Strait of Hormuz — a sliver of murky blue-green water caught between brown scorched coastline and heat shimmer haze, the ghost silhouette of an anchored Aframax product tanker riding high and empty on the horizon, its white hull dull and motionless, multiple deck pipe manifolds barely visible as dark horizontal lines, the sky above crushed into dark crimson and charcoal with oppressive petrochemical smog layering the atmosphere, the entire composition drenched in high-contrast shadow with only the gauge needle and the distant water catching any light, no text, no antialiasing, 16-bit SNES pixel art style.

dawn.driver: Largest ever map of universe captures 47 million galaxies and quasars. The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument (DESI) has finished the most detailed survey of the universe to date, and the resulting map will help researchers understand an apparent weakening of dark energy.
dawn.blurb: 47 MILLION GALAXIES. HUMANITY MAPS THE COSMOS.
dawn.summary: Even as geopolitical tensions dominate the headlines, human curiosity pressed relentlessly outward — DESI has completed the most detailed survey of the universe ever assembled, charting 47 million galaxies and quasars in a single breathtaking map. This isn't incremental science; it may finally crack the mystery of dark energy, the force shaping the fate of everything. On days when the news feels suffocating, it's worth pausing to remember that we are also the species that built an instrument capable of seeing nearly the entire observable universe.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: DESI's 47-million-galaxy map represents the largest cosmic survey in history and could resolve the dark energy mystery driving the universe's expansion.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: More than £1 billion was pledged for Sudan at the Berlin conference, exceeding the humanitarian funding target despite ongoing conflict — donors showed up.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: South Korea is turning the Iran energy crisis into a national renewables revolution, accelerating solar and wind investment with new urgency and political will.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: New research is quantifying humanity's relationship with nature in constructive terms, shifting conservation from misanthropy toward measurable coexistence.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: Scientists discovered a gene from yaks and Tibetan antelopes that helps the brain repair itself, opening a surprising new path for treating nerve damage in humans.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Silhouette composition, strong backlight from an enormous glowing wedge-shaped universe map dominating the entire interior of the Mayall Telescope dome at Kitt Peak, the fan-shaped DESI survey map blazing with rich gold and vibrant emerald light, its 47 million galaxy points clustering into luminous filaments and coral-reef structures across the wedge, warm amber and deep green radiance flooding outward and upward through the open dome aperture into the Arizona desert night sky beyond. The dome opening frames an impossibly star-dense sky, real stars bleeding into the map's artificial ones. In pure black silhouette against this golden-emerald blaze: a lone researcher stands before towering monitors displaying the luminous wedge, arms slightly raised toward the screen, their entire figure rimmed in warm gold backlight, unrecognizable as individual, universal as discovery. At the circular focal plane overhead, the disk of 5,000 robotic fiber-optic positioners catches the emerald glow like a mechanical sunflower face suspended in shadow. Bundles of fiber optic cables cascade downward in silhouette. Secondary silhouettes of graduate students at workstations flank the central figure, faces invisible, only the glow of monitors and the great map illuminating their outlines. The observatory floor reflects pools of gold and green light. Saturated, alive, afternoon-sun-through-leaves richness in every photon of the map's impossible reach.
